briefing.opening: Good morning. Let's begin the climate briefing for May 13, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Advancements in AI-Powered Climate Crisis Prediction and Policy Solutions 🌱
sections.content: Today, several positive reports have emerged domestically regarding the development of climate crisis prediction and response solutions utilizing Artificial Intelligence (AI). KAIST, through international collaborative research, is developing an integrated climate research model that utilizes AI to consider economic and social impacts [3, 6, 12, 13, 25, 38, 40]. This contributes to the realization of a 'virtual policy laboratory' that analyzes thousands of policy scenarios in minutes and simultaneously predicts climate crises and economic impacts [10, 22, 33, 35, 36, 39]. Notably, KAIST has proposed the world's first 'AI Foundation Model' to offer solutions for the climate crisis [27], opening new horizons in future climate crisis prediction, policy analysis, and the provision of response solutions [4, 17, 20, 23].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Health and Ecosystem Impacts of Climate Change and Domestic Responses ⚠️
sections.content: Amid concerns about the impacts of climate change on human health and ecosystems, various response efforts are continuing both domestically and internationally. The Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency (KDCA) has launched its second expert committee to strengthen health impact assessments of climate change [2, 5, 8, 9, 30], and research to find scientific evidence for 'climate depression' caused by heatwaves and cold snaps is also planned [7, 37]. Meanwhile, the climate crisis is directly impacting the agricultural and food sectors, not only affecting efforts to expand consumption of Jeju blueberries and kiwis [18] but also altering the flavor of tea, making it more bitter [19]. Furthermore, Korea is expanding its efforts for ecosystem protection, including strengthening international cooperation to protect salmon in the era of climate change at the North Pacific Anadromous Fish Commission (NPAFC) meeting [14].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Efforts by Domestic Local Governments and Businesses to Achieve Carbon Neutrality 🌱
sections.content: Various domestic local governments and businesses are taking concrete actions to achieve carbon neutrality. Gwangmyeong City is strengthening its smart city foundation by promoting the establishment of a 'Data Station' and 'Integrated Platform' to manage dispersed carbon neutrality data [43, 54, 55, 57, 58, 59, 65, 72, 82, 89, 92, 93, 96, 97], while Jeonju City and Cheongju City are strengthening the greenhouse gas reduction capabilities of environmental basic facilities and promoting projects to achieve 2050 carbon neutrality [24, 60, 81]. LH is showcasing AI-based carbon-neutral apartment complexes and residential technologies [44, 46, 61, 63, 70, 71, 75, 91], and Hanmi Pharmaceutical has achieved the feat of being included in the global ESG index for two consecutive years, receiving high evaluations in carbon neutrality and health management [32, 48, 79, 88]. In addition, efforts from various entities continue, including Uiwang City's volunteer activities for heavy rain damage prevention [16, 28], Changwon City's expansion of climate-response urban forest creation [85], and ICLEI Korea Office's carbon neutrality capacity-building education [47, 76].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Facing Challenges 🌱
sections.content: Domestic efforts to expand renewable energy are actively underway, but at the same time, challenges that need to be addressed have also been highlighted. Kookmin Solar, in collaboration with HRE, has signed Korea's first gigawatt (GW)-scale renewable energy co-development agreement, contributing to the spread of 'citizen-led public development' [98, 102, 110], and Korea Southern Power (KOSPO) is leading public-led solar power dissemination, including launching a 6GW solar roadmap utilizing industrial complex rooftops in cooperation with the Korea Industrial Complex Corporation and Shinhan Asset Management [108, 111, 116, 117, 118, 127, 128, 130, 132, 133, 134]. Furthermore, the Gyeongbuk Provincial Office of Education has selected 30 schools as 'Sunshine Connection Schools' to install solar power [120], and Jeollanam-do Province is requesting national funding for resident-participatory renewable energy projects [103, 119, 125, 129, 131], showing movements to expand renewable energy in various regions. However, the lack of effectiveness in Korea's renewable energy transition, which is at the 'bottom of the OECD' level [105], criticisms that compensation for fossil fuel power generation delays the renewable energy transition [107], and issues between increasing renewable energy facilities and aging existing facilities [100, 114] remain important challenges to be addressed.

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Awareness of Climate Change and Its Widespread Impacts ⚠️
sections.content: In the international community, deep concerns about climate change are being reported, along with its widespread impacts. According to public opinion polls, most people are concerned about climate change [138], which is profoundly affecting ecosystems worldwide, restructuring social structures in Central America [137], threatening tens of thousands of plant species [141], and preventing bees from finding flowers [145]. Furthermore, alongside the impacts of war and climate change on food security [146], researchers are warning that a year of severe fires due to climate change could be approaching [144]. Past climate change events, such as the 'Year Without a Summer' in 1816 [139], remind us of the severity of the current crisis, and the briefing received by the Bangladesh delegation on climate change countermeasures [147] suggests the urgency of developing international solutions. In this situation, there is also an implication that awareness is spreading regarding the problems caused by the excesses of the capitalist system and an economic system based on the principle of profit [148].

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, climate change is profoundly impacting our society across the board, from prediction and response efforts through artificial intelligence technology to health, ecosystems, and energy systems. While various domestic and international actors are actively working towards carbon neutrality and renewable energy transition, at the same time, this is a moment that requires greater implementation and international cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.
